title: nasa rugosa subsp. gracilipes
intro: Nasa rugosa subsp. gracilipes, a species of flowering plant in the family Loasaceae, is native to the Andean region in South America. It is also known as "Cochayuyo" in some regions. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial activities, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Nasa rugosa subsp. gracilipes include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems, and it is often prepared as a herbal tea or tincture for these purposes.
